text: PostgreSQL 18.4 should remain the production baseline because 19 is still beta.
Python 3.14.6, Node 24 LTS, Next.js 16.2, and Xcode 26.6 with Swift 6.3 are current supported choices.
Explicit KGP 2.3.x with AGP 9.2 is a fully supported official combination.
gpt-5.6-sol is a public OpenAI model that supports the Responses API.
The current OpenAI account can call gpt-5.6-sol.
A reliable PostgreSQL outbox requires idempotent consumers and deterministic atomic claiming; it does not make external effects exactly once.
Python core should own domain rules while BFFs remain channel-specific public adapters rather than full backend replacements.
Migration correctness must be tested by applying reviewed Alembic history to a real fresh PostgreSQL database.
A single identical GitHub Actions localhost stack cannot be shared by Ubuntu PostgreSQL jobs and macOS iOS jobs.
The local Node and JDK versions differ from the intended Node 24 and JDK 17 toolchains.

Before real beta collection, the project must document the data and provider facts and determine which privacy, sensitive-data, processor, overseas-transfer, retention, deletion, and rights controls apply.
The service must disclose AI operation and generated outputs, and must disclose free-beta consideration when a compensated review is used promotionally.
OpenAI API no-training-by-default does not eliminate application-state and abuse-monitoring retention obligations.
Innerview should remain career decision support and avoid diagnosis, treatment, clinical scores, and medical efficacy claims.
AGP 9+ officially defaults to built-in Kotlin without a separately applied kotlin-android plugin.
